examples.title: Basic Swing Application Structure
examples.description: Demonstrates fundamental Swing components with JFrame and JButton, contrasting with AWT's heavyweight approach
examples.explanation:
1. JFrame replaces AWT Frame
2. JButton is lightweight component
3. setDefaultCloseOperation shows improved control
4. No native peer dependencies

examples.title: Lightweight Component Painting
examples.description: Demonstrates Swing's pure Java rendering without native peers using paintComponent
examples.explanation:
1. Extend JPanel for custom component
2. Override paintComponent not paint()
3. No native OS resource allocation
4. Smooth cross-platform rendering

examples.title: Algorithm: Swing Component Creation
examples.description: Generic process for creating Swing components with pluggable look-and-feel
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CreateSwingComponent
BEGIN
 1. Define component (e.g., JButton)
 2. Set initial properties (text, size)
 3. Attach to container hierarchy
 4. Register event listeners
 5. UI delegate renders component
 6. LookAndFeel manages appearance
END
examples.explanation:
Shows separation of component logic from rendering
Highlights pluggable L&F architecture

examples.title: MVC Pattern in JList
examples.explanation:
1. Model stores data separately
2. JList acts as view
3. Automatic view updates
4. Decoupled architecture
